techniques.evidence_quote: an internal compute instance that we created was accessible from the outside world and it contained an AWS API key; this compute instance was compromised
unauthorized use of an administrative API key in one of our production AWS accounts
this compute instance was compromised and the AWS API key was stolen
we created a database snapshot for testing... the AWS API key was used to access the snapshot
the data exfiltration occurred in October 2018. The dataset was from a snapshot as of September 15, 2017... email addresses, hashed and salted passwords, API keys, and TLS keys

attack_chain.description: An internal AWS compute instance, publicly reachable from the internet, was compromised by an external threat actor. The instance had been created during the 2017 AWS/RDS evaluation and migration process and was never placed behind a VPN or restricted by security group rules.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: an internal compute instance that we created was accessible from the outside world and it contained an AWS API key; this compute instance was compromised

attack_chain.technique_name: Unsecured Credentials: Cloud Instance Metadata API
attack_chain.description: After compromising the compute instance, the attacker stole an AWS administrative API key that was stored on or accessible from the instance. The key was not adequately protected or rotated.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: this compute instance was compromised and the AWS API key was stolen

attack_chain.description: The attacker used the stolen administrative AWS API key to authenticate to Imperva's production AWS account, gaining the privileges of an administrator without needing to exploit any additional vulnerability.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: unauthorized use of an administrative API key in one of our production AWS accounts

attack_chain.description: Using the administrative API key, the attacker enumerated cloud resources and located the RDS database snapshot that had been created for testing during the 2017 migration.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: we created a database snapshot for testing... the AWS API key was used to access the snapshot

attack_chain.description: The attacker accessed the RDS database snapshot and exfiltrated its contents, including email addresses, hashed and salted passwords, API keys, and TLS/SSL certificates belonging to Cloud WAF customers.
attack_chain.evidence_quote: the data exfiltration occurred in October 2018. The dataset was from a snapshot as of September 15, 2017... email addresses, hashed and salted passwords, API keys, and TLS keys

initial_access_vector: Publicly accessible internal EC2 compute instance (no VPN/security group restriction) that contained an AWS administrative API key was compromised by an external attacker
persistence_mechanism: None described; attacker used stolen long-lived administrative API key for continued access
privilege_escalation: Stolen AWS administrative API key provided immediate administrative-level access to the production AWS account without requiring additional escalation
